question_text: Name two ways that energy is wasted when an electric motor is running.
correct_answer: Heat (from electrical resistance in wires) and friction (in moving parts/bearings); accept any two from: heat, friction, sound, air resistance
explanation: Electric motors convert electrical energy into kinetic (movement) energy, but some energy is always lost. The main losses are heat generated by electrical resistance in the wires and friction between moving mechanical parts. Small amounts of energy may also be lost as sound.
